rulings.Case Title: CATHERINE HERRERA WATSON VS. TONY GARNICKI
rulings.Calendar Matter: Corrected For Discovery Dept 501 - Plaintiffs Request For Reconsideration Of Plaintiffs Motion For Leave To Amend
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 19, 2021 line 1. PLAINTIFF CATHERINE WATSON Corrected For Discovery Dept 501 - Plaintiffs Request For Reconsideration Of Plaintiffs Motion For Leave To Amend is continued to July 21, 2021 to be heard by the Honorable Charles F. Haines. =(501/HEK) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SHIRLEY YANHUA YU VS. FRANKLIN M. QUESADA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Shirley Yanhua Yu'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Santions, Or, In The Alternative To Compel Compliance With Previous Order Compelling Attendance At Deposition
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 19, 2021 line 2. Plaintiff Shirley Yanhua Yu'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Santions, Or, In The Alternative To Compel Compliance With Previous Order Compelling Attendance At Deposition Hearing Required. If defendant Franklin Mijango, a.k.a. Franklin M. Quesada does not appear or have a valid excuse for his non-appearance, terminating sanctions will be awarded against him. If he does appear, he will be given one last opportunity to have his deposition taken. =(501/HEK) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: NORMAN KLEIN VS. DAVID BOGACHIK ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication, And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 19, 2021 line 4. DEFENDANT LARYSA VOLYNETS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication is DENIED. There are triable issues of fact regarding nuisance, health and safety, waiver and retaliation. See plaintiff's material facts and facts disputing defendant's purported undisputed facts in plaintiff's response and evidence to defendant's separate statement. =(501/HEK) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
